entries.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: Technical Program Manager, Knowledge Systems
messages.message: I ran production for Base at Coinbase, so the org and its cadence are familiar ground. What pulls me toward Knowledge Systems is the AI-assisted side. I built P.H.R.E.D., an operating system for AI-assisted work that keeps output connected instead of scattered across docs and dead-end conversations: source-of-truth structure, workflow patterns, and an orchestration agent, with humans holding scope and privacy. You can see it here: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. In my experience, the hard part of RAG and agentic work isn't the output, it's giving the tools enough structure to actually help. Worth a conversation about this role?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Staff Technical Program Manager, CX Agent Experience
messages.message: I already know my way around Coinbase, I ran production for Base as a contract lead, so the pace and the internal-tooling stakes aren't new to me. This EAA role reads like my lane: I've owned program delivery on complex builds where upstream changes and compliance requirements shape every plan. At Aleo I ran a Zero Knowledge Proof product launch where KYC and regulatory compliance had to hold up alongside the game mechanics, so building for investigators and agents in a regulated environment is familiar ground. Lately I've been formalizing how AI-assisted work stays connected instead of scattered across docs, which fits the human-oversight bar here. Worth a conversation about Agent Cockpit?

messages.title: Brand Producer
messages.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
messages.message:
I'm a digital EP with 15+ years of coming into fast, fluid projects and building the structure that lets creatives just make the work. The Brand Producer scope reads like my day job: paid campaigns, web builds, lean teams, agencies and freelancers onboarded without the wheels coming off.

Recent proof: I ran the AirCover for Hosts global launch at Airbnb, and before that managed a $14MM HTC retainer with a small team at Swift. Both lived or died on timelines, dependencies, and keeping approvals moving without slowing the creative down.

Would it be worth a conversation about the Brand Studio's current slate and where a producer would plug in first?

messages.title: Senior Programs & Business Operations Lead, Airbnb Services
messages.message: I did a contract tour as Executive Producer on the AirCover for Hosts global launch, so the CBO side of Airbnb and the concept-to-scale motion are familiar ground. Taking a new offering from launch to real adoption is most of what I've done for 15+ years, whether that was a $14MM HTC retainer at Swift or standing up new programs at AKQA. What I like about this one is the emerging-category, no-direct-authority reality; in my experience that's where clean process and honest cross-functional relationships do the heavy lifting. Would it be useful to walk through how I'd stand up the program side for a new service category?

messages.title: Product Operations Manager
messages.message: The line in your posting about AI output going everywhere instead of connecting is the exact problem I got tired of watching, so I built a system to fix it. It's called P.H.R.E.D., Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery (https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred): an operating layer with an orchestration agent that keeps agentic work tied to a single source of truth instead of scattered across docs. I've spent 15+ years running launches, most recently the AirCover for Hosts global launch at AirBnB and Production Lead for Base at Coinbase, so end-to-end coordination across cross-functional teams is familiar ground. Worth a conversation about how P.H.R.E.D. maps to your launch quality goals?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Creative Director, Growth
messages.message:
I've spent 15+ years leading multidisciplinary creative teams, most recently living where growth work and AI actually collide. At Coinbase I was Production Lead for Base, and at Mission Squad I ran product and growth for an agentic AI platform, so directing work that has to explain frontier tech to real people is familiar ground.

The piece most relevant here is my Mozilla work: a Gen Z-coded, digital-plus-IRL activation for their Open What You Want campaign. https://www.randallfransen.com/work/billionaire-blast-off-mozilla In my experience, the trick is keeping speed and taste on the same side of the table.

Worth a call to see if I fit the Growth bar you're building?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Create a Gen Z-coded moment to amplify Open What You Want.

messages.title: Marketing Events Producer
messages.location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
messages.message: I've spent 15+ years as a digital executive producer running multidisciplinary teams, big budgets, and outside vendors under aggressive timelines. On AirBnB's global AirCover launch and Coinbase's Base, I owned the messy middle: production docs, vendor scoping, and keeping stakeholders and creative moving in one direction. The vendor-and-budget muscle here is real, and I've found the calm-under-pressure part is where I'm most useful. The live show-calling craft I'd bring less of on paper, so I'd rather be straight about that than oversell it. Recently I've been deep in the AI space, which is why Anthropic's stage caught my eye. Open to a quick conversation about where I'd fit best on the events team?

messages.title: Strategy & Operations Manager, Consumer Revenue
messages.message: I've spent 15+ years as a producer standing up structure where none existed, which is really the throughline of this Consumer Revenue role: turning ambiguity into process. At Swift I managed a $14MM HTC retainer with a team of 4, and at AKQA I built and ran programs for 3-4 clients with senior direct reports, defining how deals moved and what we'd greenlight. The deal-desk instinct (red lines, approval frameworks, guardrails) is familiar ground for me. I'm also deep in AI-assisted workflows now, having built an orchestration system to keep small teams' work connected instead of scattered: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. Worth a conversation about the Nitro partnerships side?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Professional Services Program Manager
messages.message:
I've spent 15+ years running billable, multi-workstream engagements where the client, the creative team, and the timeline all had to stay aligned, so the shape of this Professional Services role is familiar ground. At Swift I managed a $14MM HTC retainer with a team of four, and as Program Director at AKQA I ran programs of three to four clients with senior direct reports. Bringing structure to complex work is what I like most: milestones, dependencies, and stakeholders who need visibility without being managed loudly.

On the AI-as-productivity-multiplier point, I've built the operating structure that keeps AI-assisted work connected instead of scattered across a dozen docs. Happy to walk you through how I'd approach the first big program. Worth a conversation?

messages.title: Viral Creative Producer
messages.message: The Brian-in-a-glass-box stunt is the kind of thing I keep sending to people, so seeing you hiring a producer for the next batch got my attention. I've spent 15+ years running digital production end to end: talent, vendors, budgets, distribution. On Mozilla's Open What You Want campaign I built a Gen Z-coded moment that connected digital and IRL over a three-month run (https://www.randallfransen.com/work/billionaire-blast-off-mozilla), which is the closest cousin I've got to earned-attention stunt work. I've also owned $4MM+ in production budgets, so real budget behind a bigger swing is where I do my best work. Worth a call to trade stunt ideas?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Create a Gen Z-coded moment to amplify Open What You Want.

messages.title: Head of Copy, Creative Studio
messages.location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
messages.message: I've spent 15+ years running digital creative teams as an executive producer, and lately I've gone deep on one problem: how AI-assisted work scales without the output scattering everywhere. Your line about being the connective tissue between human creative vision and AI execution infrastructure is exactly where I've been living. I built P.H.R.E.D., a project OS with an orchestration agent that keeps AI work connected to real decisions instead of dead-ending in a doc: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. I've also produced brand launches at Coinbase, Yuga, and Aleo. Worth a conversation about the AI-execution side of this role?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Chief of Staff, to the CPO
messages.message: I've spent 15+ years as an executive producer keeping leadership teams aligned and quietly on-task, and the AI-first piece of this role is where I actually live right now. At Mission Squad I was Director of Product & Growth for an agentic AI platform, and I built P.H.R.E.D. (https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred), an operating system that keeps AI-assisted work connected instead of scattered across a dozen docs. In my experience most AI adoption fails not on output but on structure, so I build the source-of-truth first. I've run programs of 3-4 clients with senior direct reports and managed a $14MM retainer, so operating cadence and cross-functional alignment are familiar ground. Worth a conversation about how I'd approach the People org's AI transformation?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery
